_description: Polarity and cable setup for each welding process. Extracted from manual pages 13, 24, 27 and the Quick Start Guide.

MIG.electrode_cable: Wire Feed Power Cable into Positive socket (+)
MIG.shielding_gas: Required — C25 (75% Argon/25% CO2) or CO2
MIG.notes: Also connect MIG Gun to wire feed, and Wire Feed Control Cable inside welder
steps: Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Negative (–) Socket — twist clockwise to lock
Plug Wire Feed Power Cable into Positive (+) Socket — twist clockwise to lock
Connect MIG Gun to Wire Feed mechanism
Connect Wire Feed Control Cable to interior socket
Connect shielding gas hose to regulator and welder gas inlet
Set gas flow to 20-30 SCFH

Flux-Cored.electrode_cable: Wire Feed Power Cable into Negative socket (–)
Flux-Cored.notes: Opposite polarity from MIG — critical difference. No gas needed.
steps: Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Positive (+) Socket — twist clockwise to lock
Plug Wire Feed Power Cable into Negative (–) Socket — twist clockwise to lock
Connect MIG Gun to Wire Feed mechanism
Connect Wire Feed Control Cable to interior socket
No shielding gas required

TIG.electrode_cable: TIG Torch Cable into Negative socket (–)
TIG.foot_pedal: Optional — connects to Foot Pedal Socket inside welder
TIG.notes: Foot pedal sold separately. TIG torch sold separately. For AC TIG (aluminum), different setting required.
steps: Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Positive (+) Socket — twist clockwise to lock
Plug TIG Torch Cable into Negative (–) Socket — twist clockwise to lock
Connect Foot Pedal Cable to Foot Pedal Socket inside welder (if using)
Connect 100% Argon gas cylinder with regulator
Connect Shielding Gas Hose to Regulator outlet and welder Gas Inlet
Set gas flow to 10-25 SCFH
Leave Wire Feed Power Cable disconnected

Stick.electrode_cable: Electrode Holder Cable into Positive socket (+)
Stick.notes: No gas, no wire feed. Most straightforward setup.
steps: Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Negative (–) Socket — twist clockwise to lock
Plug Electrode Holder Cable into Positive (+) Socket — twist clockwise to lock
Leave Wire Feed Power Cable disconnected
No shielding gas required

socket_layout.description: Front panel socket layout (left to right): MIG Gun/Spool Gun Cable Socket, Negative Socket (–), Positive Socket (+)
socket_layout.negative_socket: Left side — accepts ground clamp for MIG/Stick, TIG torch for TIG
socket_layout.positive_socket: Right side — accepts wire feed power for MIG, ground clamp for TIG/Flux-Cored, electrode holder for Stick
